title: Competency Inventory and Independent Evidence Ledger · Career Evidence Case: Evidence Strength
summary: A candidate lists technologies and course completions, yet cannot show the behavior, changed case, personal contribution, limitation, or current reproducibility behind any claim. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the evidence strength increment in competency-to-evidence ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability.
Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Evidence Strength · Theory Evidence Strength
steps.instruction:
theory case — A course-rich skills list cannot identify one independently reproduced behavior, changed case, personal contribution, current artifact, or honest limitation. The theory variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Evidence Strength contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Evidence Strength · Theory Evidence Strength.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability. For Predict Evidence Strength · Theory Evidence Strength, keep Evidence Strength connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A course-rich skills list cannot identify one independently reproduced behavior, changed case, personal contribution, current artifact, or honest limitation. The theory variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Evidence Strength condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: EEOC Employment Discrimination and AI for Workers — Controls awareness that automated recruiting, screening, assessment, promotion, and pay tools can implicate protected rights and accommodation needs.

hints: For Predict Evidence Strength · Theory Evidence Strength, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Evidence Strength precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Predict Evidence Strength · Theory Evidence Strength, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A course-rich skills list cannot identify one independently reproduced behavior, changed case, personal contribution, current artifact, or honest limitation. The theory variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability.

For Predict Evidence Strength · Theory Evidence Strength, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A polished final artifact proves every skill used to create it.

options.text: A polished final artifact proves every skill used to create it. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Evidence Strength.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.text: Rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A course-rich skills list cannot identify one independently reproduced behavior, changed case, personal contribution, current artifact, or honest limitation. The worked example variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability. A defensible solution applies Evidence Strength, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to competency-to-evidence ledger.

content.code:
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-3
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A polished final artifact proves every skill used to create it. This fails the Evidence Strength contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: EEOC Employment Discrimination and AI for Workers (Worker resource published 2024; reviewed 2026-07-15) — Controls awareness that automated recruiting, screening, assessment, promotion, and pay tools can implicate protected rights and accommodation needs.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
